chapter: Chapter: CONNECTING THE DOTS (BASICS OF GEOMETRY)

questions.question: What is a 'Point' in geometry?
questions.answer: A point is a location in space that has no size (no length, width, or height).

questions.question: Define a 'Line Segment'.
questions.answer: A line segment is a part of a line that has two fixed endpoints.

questions.question: How many lines can pass through a single point?
questions.answer: An infinite number of lines.

questions.question: How many lines can pass through two distinct points?
questions.answer: Exactly one line.

questions.question: What is a 'Ray'?
questions.answer: A ray is a part of a line that has one endpoint and extends infinitely in the other direction.

questions.question: What are 'Intersecting Lines'?
questions.answer: Two lines that meet at exactly one point are called intersecting lines.

questions.question: Define 'Parallel Lines'.
questions.answer: Lines in a plane that never meet, no matter how far they are extended, are called parallel lines.

questions.question: What is an 'Angle'?
questions.answer: An angle is formed by two rays starting from a common initial point.

questions.question: What is the common endpoint of an angle called?
questions.answer: The Vertex.

questions.question: What is an 'Acute Angle'?
questions.answer: An angle whose measure is less than 90°.

questions.question: What is an 'Obtuse Angle'?
questions.answer: An angle whose measure is greater than 90° but less than 180°.

questions.question: Define a 'Right Angle'.
questions.answer: An angle whose measure is exactly 90°.

questions.question: What are 'Collinear Points'?
questions.answer: Three or more points that lie on the same straight line.

questions.question: What is a 'Triangle'?
questions.answer: A closed figure made up of three line segments.

questions.question: How many vertices does a triangle have?
questions.answer: Three vertices.

questions.question: What is an 'Equilateral Triangle'?
questions.answer: A triangle in which all three sides are of equal length.

questions.question: What is an 'Isosceles Triangle'?
questions.answer: A triangle that has at least two equal sides.

questions.question: What is a 'Scalene Triangle'?
questions.answer: A triangle in which all three sides have different lengths.

questions.question: What is the sum of the angles in a triangle?
What is a 'Straight Angle'?
questions.answer: An angle whose measure is exactly 180°.

questions.question: Define 'Concurrent Lines'.
questions.answer: Three or more lines that pass through the same point.

questions.question: What is the instrument used to measure an angle?
questions.answer: A Protractor.

questions.question: What is a 'Closed Curve'?
questions.answer: A curve that starts and ends at the same point.

questions.question: What is a 'Polygon'?
questions.answer: A simple closed curve made up of only line segments.

questions.question: What is a 'Reflex Angle'?
questions.answer: An angle which is greater than 180° and less than 360°.
